title: Unit, Integration, CLI, Fixture, and Changed-Case Testing · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent archive team receives a different authorized corpus, platform, language mix, file size, report consumer, terminal width, error policy, and delivery constraint and transfers unit, integration, cli, fixture, and changed-case testing into a changed risk-layered Bookbot test suite. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of risk-layered Bookbot test suite with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Partition tests across pure units, file integration, CLI behavior, report snapshots, package installation, and stakeholder acceptance.
Use named table and subtest cases for empty, punctuation, Unicode, malformed, large, tied, and boundary inputs.
Use real temporary files and directories for filesystem contracts and reserve mocks for narrow failure injection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Test Partitions
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 5263 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer unit, real-file integration, CLI, changed-defect, and installed smoke evidence; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Partition tests across pure units, file integration, CLI behavior, report snapshots, package installation, and stakeholder acceptance.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Test Partitions.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for partition tests across pure units, file integration, cli behavior, report snapshots, package installation, and stakeholder acceptance. For Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Test Partitions, keep Test Partitions connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-test-quality-uc8b1l" that uses case 7126 to layer unit, real-file integration, CLI, changed-defect, and installed smoke evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-test-quality-uc8b1l
# Competency: bookbot-test-quality.
# Case 7126: a multilingual community archive intake.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_test_quality_uc8b1l(unit_cases: int = 18, integration_cases: int = 7, cli_cases: int = 8, changed_defect_detected: bool = True, installed_smoke: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_uc8b1l = "uc8b1l-l1b8cu-7126"
    assert evidence_variant_uc8b1l
    evidence_axes_uc8b1l = (
        "corpusuc8b1l",
        "policyl1b8cu",
        "revision7126",
        "commanduc8b1l7126",
        "reportl1b8cu7126",
        "test7126uc8b1l",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_uc8b1l)) == 6
    if min(unit_cases, integration_cases, cli_cases) < 1:
        return False, 0, "test-layers"
    if integration_cases < 2:
        return False, integration_cases, "filesystem-evidence"
    if cli_cases < 2:
        return False, cli_cases, "cli-evidence"
    if not changed_defect_detected or not installed_smoke:
        return False, unit_cases, "weak-suite"
    total = unit_cases + integration_cases + cli_cases
    assert total >= 7
    return True, total, "risk-layered-tests"
